stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 investment portfolio options. The probability of favorable market conditions is uncertain, estimated between 41% and 55%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: bonds: $75K in good market conditions, $14K in bad market conditions. index fund: $195K in good market conditions, $99K in bad market conditions. tech stocks: $56K in good market conditions, $15K in bad market conditions. real estate: $146K in good market conditions, $39K in bad market conditions. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.48: best option is index fund with EV=$145.1K. index fund is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [138.4, 151.8].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
index fund is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
